commands.repair_reason: Protected MEMBER security/maintainer PR with no narrow automation repair remaining; next action is maintainer security review and broad CI.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the inspected diff; the remaining issue is expected human review for a security-sensitive authority model change. (sha=0d39f5603eecc8beb7d2f73220dce55016fc1422)
commands.review_summary:
This PR replaces model-visible system-event trust handling with explicit owner-authority metadata across queued event producers, focused tests, and OpenGrep coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a user bug. The relevant behavior is source-verifiable: queued authority metadata is explicit at producers and is consumed by existing reply and heartbeat owner-downgrade paths.
commands.review_followup: Protected MEMBER security/maintainer PR with no narrow automation repair remaining; next action is maintainer security review and broad CI.

target.title: Remove system event trust metadata
target.body:
## Summary

- Remove model-visible queued system-event trust labels: generated event context now uses the normal `System:` prefix instead of `System (untrusted):`.
- Preserve non-owner authority behavior through structured runtime metadata: queued events can request `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true`, and reply/heartbeat run construction forwards that to the existing owner-authority controls.
- Keep compatibility for installed plugins that still pass `trusted: false`: enqueue-time compatibility maps it to non-owner authority, but prompt rendering does not branch on trust.
- Preserve older admitted host behavior for published channel plugins by dual-writing deprecated `trusted: false` wherever those plugins emit an event with `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true`.
- Replace the OpenGrep trust-flag rule with a rule that checks explicit authority metadata instead of requiring a `trusted` prompt-label flag.

## Why This Removes The Old Security Feature

The old `trusted` system-event feature mixed content provenance with runtime authority. That was the wrong abstraction: nearly all context the model reads can be untrusted, including channel threads, GitHub issues, web pages, files, tool output, search results, quoted messages, and channel history. A narrow queued-event label could not be a real prompt-injection boundary.

The part worth preserving is not the `System (untrusted):` string. The part worth preserving is that event-triggered runs from webhooks, reactions, node notifications, async exec completions, task events, cron awareness, and similar automation should not silently inherit owner authority when the run source is not the owner. This PR keeps that behavior through structured metadata instead of prompt text.

## Authority Behavior

- Reply runs no longer parse `System (untrusted):` or any other rendered prompt text to decide `senderIsOwner`.
- `drainFormattedSystemEventBlock()` returns both formatted text and `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse`; `runPreparedReply()` uses that structured boolean to set `senderIsOwner=false` and matching trace authorization.
- Heartbeat runs inspect queued event metadata for `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse`, while exec completion heartbeats remain explicit non-owner runs.
- Current producers that previously relied on `trusted: false` now use `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true`; intentionally owner-inheriting internal events use `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: false`.
- Dedupe/equality includes the authority bit so an owner-inheriting event cannot consume or mask a non-owner event with the same text/context.

## Compatibility

`trusted?: boolean` remains only as a deprecated input adapter on the system-event enqueue options. Legacy `trusted: false` still maps to non-owner runtime authority for third-party/plugin compatibility, but OpenClaw does not render it as a model-visible trust label.

Published channel plugin producers that are still admitted by older host floors now dual-write `trusted: false` alongside `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true`. That keeps the previous downgrade behavior for older hosts that only understand the legacy key, without reintroducing prompt-label trust handling in current hosts. This is intentionally limited to downgrade events; owner-inheriting events do not get a legacy untrusted marker.

This intentionally avoids unrelated historical cleanup: old archived `System (untrusted):` strings remain historical strings. The PR does not add transcript rewriting or old-log migration logic just to hide the old label.

## Security History / Review Note

This replaces behavior that previously landed as security hardening, so it should be reviewed as a security posture change rather than a pure cleanup. The old hardening made some event-triggered runs non-owner, but it did so through a prompt-visible trusted/untrusted abstraction. This PR preserves the non-owner behavior through explicit runtime metadata and removes the label as a security boundary.

If future product policy wants more event authority control, it should live at ingress authorization, tool policy, explicit run construction, or a user/operator-controlled event policy. It should not depend on model-visible strings in the prompt.

## GitHub Review Notes

- ClawSweeper flagged that published plugins with older admitted host floors would lose downgrade behavior if the PR removed `trusted: false` from plugin producer calls before those host floors were raised.
- This PR addresses that by retaining deprecated `trusted: false` at published plugin producer boundaries where the event is explicitly downgraded with `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true`.
- Stale GitHub Advanced Security/OpenGrep comments still mention the old missing-`trusted` rule; the PR replaces that rule with explicit authority metadata enforcement.

## Diff Size Reconciliation

The current PR diff from `origin/main` is `57 files changed, 345 insertions(+), 116 deletions(-)`. The additions are focused tests, explicit authority annotations where existing behavior needs to be preserved, published-plugin compatibility dual-writes, and the OpenGrep authority-rule replacement. The removed surface includes current producer reliance on prompt trust labels, trust-label rendering, and the old trust-flag rule.

## Review

- Re-read and followed the updated parent review workflow: surface mapping, rubbish/churn audit, independent review, finding discipline, validation, and final closeout on the branch.
- Rubbish audit removed an unnecessary legacy archived-transcript fixture; this PR does not try to clean historical logs.
- Final closeout review on the earlier pushed head reported no accepted/actionable findings; the later compatibility commits are intentionally narrow and covered by focused channel tests, lint, and test typecheck.

## Verification

- `fnm exec --using 22 pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `fnm exec --using 22 pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 $(git show --name-only --format='' HEAD)`
- `fnm exec --using 22 pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `fnm exec --using 22 pnpm check:test-types`
- `fnm exec --using 22 pnpm test extensions/discord/src/monitor/monitor.agent-components.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.preflight.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/channels.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/reactions.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/messages.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/pins.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/members.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/events/interactions.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/reaction-events.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/interactions.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reactions.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/reaction-handler.test.ts` (5 Vitest shards, 425 tests)
- `fnm exec --using 22 pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/interactions.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.thread-parent.test.ts` (2 Vitest shards, 57 tests)
- `node security/opengrep/check-rule-metadata.mjs security/opengrep/precise.yml`
- `opengrep validate security/opengrep/precise.yml` (passed with existing nonfatal rule warnings)
- `OPENCLAW_OPENGREP_BASE_REF=$(git merge-base origin/main HEAD) scripts/run-opengrep.sh --changed --error` (0 findings)
- Earlier branch proof before the compatibility commit also ran `node scripts/check-changelog-attributions.mjs CHANGELOG.md`, `fnm exec --using 22 pnpm test <26 changed test files>` across 12 Vitest shards, `fnm exec --using 22 pnpm test src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts`, and a single-rule OpenGrep scan over `src`, `extensions`, and `packages` with 0 findings.

Known current gate note: the CI dependency/dead-code shard reproduced locally with `Unexpected unused files: extensions/qa-lab/src/tool-coverage-report.ts`. That file is already on `origin/main` and is not changed by this PR.

Not run locally on this final head: broad `pnpm check` / full `pnpm test`; CI should provide the broad gate after this push.
